meta.title: Alexis Herrera Saucedo | Frontend Developer — Vue, React, Next.js
meta.description: Frontend developer with 4+ years in POS systems, government statistics platforms, and fintech back offices. Vue, React, Next.js, TypeScript. Core Web Vitals and technical SEO. Mexico · remote.
meta.keywords: Alexis Herrera Saucedo, frontend developer, Vue.js, React, Next.js, TypeScript, Nuxt.js, POS, fintech, SICT, remote Mexico

job1.description:
• Two-person frontend team building a back office for a digital wallet platform (Vue 2 + Nuxt.js + Vuex + Vuetify), with three roles: General Admin, Company Admin, and Subcompany Admin.
• Built movement and transaction views segmented by entity level (company, subcompany, user), with activity feeds contextualized by role.
• Developed user detail views: credential management (email, password), physical card assignment, data display, and blocking — each action with its own validation flow.
• Built card ordering flows and a disputes module so admins can raise, track, and resolve incidents.
• Managed shared state with Vuex and REST integration via Axios, coordinating with the other dev to keep data flow consistent.
Achievements:
• Delivered 5 modules covering all three admin roles as part of a two-person team.
• Aligned Vuex structure and component conventions from the start, avoiding rework as the platform grew.

job2.description:
• Team project: POS cash management system integrated with NetSuite ERP for a restaurant chain. Covers end-of-shift cash count, terminal batch closing, coupon accrual, and bank reports.
• Built coupon accrual screens — display, validation, and connection to NetSuite sales order generation.
• Developed correspondent bank reports (Santander, Banamex, Scotiabank) and coupon annexes, summarizing data by payment type and currency per shift.
• Built reusable components adopted across modules, keeping UI patterns consistent and reducing repeated work.
Achievements:
• Coupon and report screens connect directly to NetSuite, giving supervisors accurate closing figures without manual entry between systems.

job3.description:
• Frontend built from scratch for the official transport statistics system of SICT (Ministry of Infrastructure, Communications and Transportation). Handles bulk Excel upload for road networks, rail, and federal motor transport; generates annual reports and stores historical records from 1990 to 2023.
• Structured the frontend with reusable components from day one, standardizing data display and form patterns across modules.
• Built the bulk Excel upload flow wired to the reporting pipeline — data is processed and reports become available for automatic download.
• Added on-screen statistics views so admins can query historical figures by year without generating a report each time.
Achievements:
• PDF title configuration module — admins define report headers from the platform without manual document editing.

job4.description:
• Ecommerce and service platforms with React, Next.js (SSR), and TypeScript for multiple clients. Focus on payment integrations, CMS connection, and SEO.
• Integrated Stripe and Square with secure session handling for two production ecommerce clients.
• Connected frontends to Strapi CMS via REST — clients manage catalogs, banners, and stock without dev team intervention.
• Applied SSR and structured metadata to improve Core Web Vitals and indexability on client sites.
Achievements:
• Codisa: Strapi CMS integration so the client manages inventory and promotions autonomously, removing dev dependency for content updates.
• Ourmaids: SSR-first restructure that improved search visibility and content hierarchy.
• Forprint: internal payments view that reduced manual reporting effort for finance.

job5.description:
• Production platform with Vue 2 + Nuxt.js + Vuex + GraphQL; also led the frontend team.
• Implemented GraphQL subscriptions for real-time notifications, replacing polling and reducing latency for critical events.
• Translated Figma prototypes to production UI and performed code reviews to maintain team quality.
• Managed task distribution and defined architecture guidelines as Frontend Lead.
Achievements:
• Internal verification and reports page that centralized admin flows and reduced manual review.
• SEO and performance optimizations: consistent load times and better organic indexability.

technicalTests.title: Technical tests
technicalTests.subtitle: Exercises framed the same way: the problem, your role, why that stack, and what they demonstrate.

hero.intro: Frontend developer with 4+ years building interfaces where data accuracy and complex flows matter: POS, government statistics systems, and financial back offices. I turn business logic into clear, maintainable UI with Vue and React.
hero.highlights: 4+ years · POS · SICT · Fintech · Vue · React · TypeScript

about.title: About
about.paragraph1: Frontend developer with 4+ years building interfaces for systems where data precision and complex flows matter: POS platforms, government statistics systems, and financial back offices. I focus on turning complex business logic into clear, reliable, easy-to-maintain UI.
about.paragraph2: My main stack is Vue and React; I care about the details that keep a project healthy: reusable components, clean state management, and consistent API integration. I also apply technical SEO and performance best practices (Core Web Vitals, SSR/SSG, structured metadata).
about.paragraph3: Available for remote and hybrid work from Mexico. I work closely with backend and product to ship business-aligned, production-ready UI.
about.paragraph4: Education: B.S. Computer Systems Engineering — National Polytechnic Institute (IPN), Mexico City, 2017–2022.

projects.title: Projects
projects.narrativeIntro: Each case summarizes the product or client problem, my role, the technical choice (and why over alternatives), and the outcome or impact direction.

codisa.title: CODISA E-Commerce
codisa.technologies: Vue · Nuxt.js · Vuetify · Pinia · GraphQL Apollo
codisa.description:
Problem: the business needed their e‑commerce to evolve without every catalog, banner, or promo change requiring a dev deployment.

Role: I led the frontend redesign and backend/CMS integration so marketing and operations could move more autonomously.

Technical decision: Nuxt and Vue with Pinia and GraphQL Apollo for fast catalog views and consistent product data; headless/CMS instead of hardcoding content in the repo because it scales better as SKUs and campaigns grow.

Outcome: the client can manage inventory and promotions with less dependency on the dev team for frequent content changes.

ourmaids.title: OurMaids
ourmaids.description:
Problem: the site needed better search visibility and clearer information hierarchy so the service was understandable and converted better.

Role: frontend redesign with an SSR-first approach and payment integration (Square) in the new architecture.

Technical decision: Next.js with React instead of a pure SPA to ship meaningful HTML from the server and tune metadata per route (stronger base for LCP and SEO); Tailwind to iterate the landing quickly; TypeScript for stable contracts with payment APIs.

Outcome: structure aligned with organic discovery and a clearer message for users; the stack favors perceived performance and indexing over a monolithic client-only app.

forprint.title: Forprint Backoffice
forprint.description:
Problem: finance spent time assembling reports and reviewing payments across disconnected tools instead of deciding in one place.

Role: I designed and built the internal panel to view transfers, accept or decline payouts, and support invoicing flows.

Technical decision: Nuxt + Vuetify + Pinia because back offices are table- and form-heavy; a mature UI kit saves time versus hand-building every control; Pinia fits this scope better than Vuex.

Outcome: less manual reporting effort and transaction decisions consolidated in a single interface.

gmarLanding.title: Give Me A Ride Landing Page
gmarLanding.description:
Problem: the landing had to build trust (plans, FAQ, legal) and stay discoverable without sacrificing clarity on mobile.

Role: I designed and built the marketing page and legal sections.

Technical decision: Nuxt over a Vue SPA-only setup for SSR and route-level metadata; Vuetify for consistent layout and forms; GraphQL where the product already consumed APIs that way, avoiding a parallel REST layer just for marketing.

Outcome: an indexable foundation and a clear product story with less friction than a static site disconnected from the rest of the stack.

gmarBackoffice.title: Give Me A Ride Backoffice
gmarBackoffice.description:
Problem: the admin team needed verification and reporting without constant full refreshes or scattered sources.

Role: I built the internal verification flows and integrated real-time notifications.

Technical decision: GraphQL subscriptions instead of polling for critical events — lower latency and less wasteful traffic; Vuex where admin state had to be shared across modules already tied to the existing stack.

Outcome: faster review flows centralized in one place instead of juggling multiple screens or repeated manual queries.

pcppd.title: PCPPD
pcppd.description:
Problem (exercise): demonstrate collaborative coding with GitHub and a PR-like flow within a small scope.

Role: frontend with repository integration and team views.

Technical decision: Vue + Firebase for quick auth and data in a demo without a custom backend; Vuex where session/team state had to be explicit instead of prop drilling only.

Outcome: a prototype that validates the collaborative flow defined for the exercise, not a production product.

todo.title: Todo List
todo.description:
Problem (exercise): show clean state and form handling in Vue 3 with modern tooling.

Role: end-to-end CRUD for tasks.

Technical decision: Pinia over Vuex for a simpler API in a small app; Vite for fast dev feedback; Vuetify to avoid reinventing base UI.

Outcome: a clear demo of patterns I reuse in larger systems.

ecommerce.title: Ecommerce
ecommerce.description:
Problem (exercise): admin catalog with persistence without deploying a custom backend.

Role: product CRUD and admin flow.

Technical decision: Angular + PrimeNG for form-heavy back-office patterns; Firebase as BaaS instead of a bespoke API under a short deadline.

Outcome: a deployable app that demonstrates data modeling and a complete admin flow within exercise scope.

lacasagallery.title: La Casa Gallery
lacasagallery.description:
Problem: sell art and gifts online with a blog and shipping without running servers or payments from scratch.

Role: Shopify theme work and Liquid components only where the design required it.

Technical decision: Shopify over a full custom headless storefront to shorten maintenance and use proven checkout/hosting; Liquid only where the base theme was not enough (collections, specific blocks).

Outcome: operations centered on Shopify with targeted customization instead of reimplementing catalog, tax, and checkout logistics.

roadStats.title: Statistics Information System
roadStats.description:
Problem: SICT needed digitized transport statistics with a 1990–2023 history, bulk Excel ingestion, and official reports without re-editing PDFs by hand every time.

Role: frontend from scratch — uploads, on-screen queries by year, and report output configuration.

Technical decision: Vue 2 + TypeScript + Pinia + Bootstrap for fast institutional forms and tables; Axios to plug into processing and downloads; TypeScript over plain JS given the data-model volume.

Outcome: configurable PDF titles from the app; historical figures viewable without generating a report on every screen; less manual document editing.

fintechBackoffice.title: Fintech Backoffice (Wallet)
fintechBackoffice.technologies: Vue 2 · Nuxt.js 2 · JavaScript · Vuetify · Axios · Vuex
fintechBackoffice.description:
Problem: run a digital wallet across general, company, subcompany, and end-user levels without losing traceability or mixing permissions; card orders and disputes had to stay in one operational context.

Role: frontend on a two-person team — movements and transactions by entity, user detail (credentials, physical card, blocking), orders, and a disputes module; Vuex and REST with Axios aligned with the other developer.

Technical decision: Vue 2 + Nuxt 2 + Vuetify + Vuex because the back office already followed that stack and the form/table density fits Vuetify; Vuex instead of isolated components only to share admin state without massive prop drilling; REST with an explicit contract where the backend did not expose GraphQL.

Outcome: five modules aligned with three admin roles from conventions agreed up front; each sensitive action has its validation; less rework as the platform grew.

contact.title: Contact
contact.text: Open to new opportunities and collaborations. Feel free to reach out via the form below or connect on
contact.formSuccess: Thanks! I'll get back to you soon.
